title: Regulation-and-event gated specialists
summary: FIA rules create structurally different stop mechanisms: dry races require two slick specs, wet races waive that requirement, 2025 Monaco had a special mandatory two-stop rule, and pre-season testing is not a normal Grand Prix.
rationale: FIA rules create structurally different stop mechanisms: dry races require two slick specs, wet races waive that requirement, 2025 Monaco had a special mandatory two-stop rule, and pre-season testing is not a normal Grand Prix. Treating these rows as one regime likely blurs distinct label-generating processes.
implementation_hint: Add explicit flags for slick-obligation-active, wet-exception-active, Monaco-2025 special rule, and testing_event. Train at least two specialists: dry strategic stops vs wet/testing/special-rule stops, then gate or blend by the rule/event flags instead of a single global model.
expected_effect: Lower CV/LB mismatch and cleaner handling of rare but structurally different subsets.
risk: Subset models can overfit because some regimes are small; keep the gating simple and back off to the global model when sample counts are low.
